title: Position Management Suite — Pyramiding, Scale-Out, Between-Scan Agents
author: Cowork
summary: Three future enhancements to active position management, deferred after the ATR trailing stop shipped (2026-03-31). Research synthesis from quantitative literature and codebase analysis. Each feature has its own backlog item (BACK-012, BACK-013, BACK-014).

pyramiding.evidence_strength: Conditional — strong in trending markets, harmful in choppy
pyramiding.key_finding: Works best with decreasing layer sizes (50/30/20%) and strict rule: new layer's risk must be covered by existing unrealised P&L. Modern algo implementations use rule-based triggers, not discretion.
pyramiding.fit_for_decifer: Low-medium. Intraday holds may not provide enough runway for 2nd layer. Activate only in BULL/MOMENTUM regimes with multi-day hold intention.

partial_scale_out.evidence_strength: Moderate — does NOT maximise raw returns but consistently improves Sharpe/Sortino
partial_scale_out.key_finding: Scale-out is about survivability and consistency, not maximising returns. Literature recommends: TP1 close 33%, TP2 close 33%, let final 34% ride with trailing stop.
partial_scale_out.fit_for_decifer: High. The 33% TP1 tier already exists. Adding TP2 + trail-the-rest is a low-effort extension of existing bracket logic.

between_scan_agents.evidence_strength: Weak at current scan frequency — belongs to HFT/execution-algo territory
between_scan_agents.key_finding: For scan-based intraday/swing systems, the scan IS the heartbeat. Between-scan agents add complexity and can conflict with the main scan pipeline. Only beneficial if upgraded to tick/1-min data.
between_scan_agents.fit_for_decifer: Low until Phase D data infrastructure upgrade.

design.trigger: Price > entry + 1 ATR AND status == ACTIVE AND regime in [BULL, MOMENTUM]
design.sizing: 2nd layer = 30% of initial qty, 3rd layer = 20% of initial qty
design.risk_rule: new_layer_risk = (new_entry - new_sl) * new_qty <= unrealised_pnl of existing position
design.stop_update: After each pyramid layer, update trailing stop to cover FULL stacked position

design.current_state: TP1: 33% sold at entry + partial_exit_1_pct (4%). No TP2. Remaining 67% has hard TP limit.
design.target_state: TP1: 33% at +4% (existing). TP2: 33% at +8% (new limit child). Remainder 34%: ATR trailing stop only, no hard TP.
design.implementation: In execute_buy(), add a 3rd SELL child to the bracket: TP2 at partial_exit_2_pct. Remaining qty gets no TP limit — protected only by ATR trail. All 3 exit legs in same OCA group.
design.notes: The math: initial qty N → tp1_qty = N//3, tp2_qty = N//3, trail_qty = N - tp1_qty - tp2_qty. Trail qty gets sl_order_id tracking (already shipping with BACK-012).

design.scope: Defensive only — no new entries. Triggers: (a) thesis-invalidation close, (b) panic-regime exit.
design.architecture: Daemon thread polling every 60s. Reads active_trades snapshot + fetches 1-min OHLCV. Runs a 2-agent mini-pipeline: ThesisValidator + RiskManager.
design.thesis_invalidation: If key support level (entry - 1.5 ATR) breaks on 1-min close, close position immediately without waiting for scan.
design.panic_exit: If VIX crosses panic threshold mid-hold, close all positions. Currently handled by check_risk_conditions() at scan time — between-scan agent closes the gap.
design.data_requirement: Needs 1-min yfinance data. Rate-limit concern: yfinance download(interval='1m') is limited to 7 days of data and has undocumented rate limits. Validate in Phase D.
design.conflict_prevention: Use existing _trades_lock (RLock) and _flatten_lock. The daemon thread never submits new buy orders — only calls execute_sell() which is already thread-safe.
design.gate: Phase C IC validation complete AND ≥200 closed trades AND 1-min data feed validated

atr_trailing_stop.description: ATR trailing stop slides stop-loss up as high-water mark advances. Stores atr, sl_order_id, high_water_mark in active_trades per position. Called each scan via update_trailing_stops(ib) in bot.py.
